title: Conjecture 1.1
statement_text: Approximating the genus of cubic graphs is APX-hard.
context_text: In the spherical density regime (graphs with average degree at most 6), there is no evidence of efficient constant-factor approximation algorithms for the genus. The second named author conjectured in prior work (2018) that approximation in this range is hard; the conjecture is stated at the outset to motivate the paper's focus on the dense-graph regime where tractable approximation is possible.
paper_contribution: States the conjecture as background motivation for focusing on the dense-graph regime; the paper does not prove or disprove it.
notes: The attributed author (Mohar, 2018) is also the second named author of this paper; the conjecture originates from a prior 2018 work, not the present paper.
paper_title: Efficient polynomial-time approximation scheme for the genus of dense graphs

title: Open Problem (Spherical Density Regime)
statement_text: Determine whether efficient constant-factor approximation algorithms exist for the genus of graphs with average degree at most 6 (spherical density regime), or prove that approximating the genus in this regime is hard.
context_text: The authors partition the genus approximation problem into three density regimes. The spherical density regime (average degree $\leq 6$) is described as 'probably most interesting'; no efficient constant-factor approximation algorithm is known for it, and the authors identify resolving this as 'the major open problem'. Conjecture 1.1 (APX-hardness for cubic graphs) is the leading candidate resolution.
notes: Stated in prose: 'resolving this is the major open problem'; no formal labelled theorem environment.
paper_title: Efficient polynomial-time approximation scheme for the genus of dense graphs
